rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydration of aryl-substituted alkenes to primary/secondary alcohols using chloride as a redox mediator and water as the hydrogen/oxygen source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of chloride (from TBAC) to Cl2/Cl+, which acts as a mediator that converts the aryl alkene to a chloriranium/chlorohydrin; cathodic reduction of a benzylic radical/cation occurs in the epoxide ring-opening sequence

key_intermediates.name: chloriranium ion (intermediate I) from aryl alkene + Cl+
benzylic radical (intermediate II) from epoxide ring-opening / SET
benzylic carbanion (intermediate III) after second cathodic SET

electron_flow: Chloride is anodically oxidized to Cl2/Cl+, which adds to the aryl alkene to form a chloriranium that is opened by H2O to chlorohydrin 5; base (K3PO4) closes 5 to epoxide 6, which is then reduced at the cathode in two sequential SET steps via a benzylic radical and carbanion that is protonated by water to deliver the anti-Markovnikov alcohol.
rate_determining_step: Cathodic reductive ring-opening of the aryl epoxide to the benzylic radical/carbanion (slow conversion of 6 to 2a observed by kinetics)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2a fully suppressed (89% 1a recovered); with epoxide 6 + TEMPO, the TEMPO–benzylic radical adduct was detected by HRMS, confirming a benzylic radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Chlorohydrin 5 (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions, gave 2a in 66%, confirming 5 as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Epoxide 6 (as substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions, gave 2a in 82%, confirming 6 as an on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: D-2a obtained in 60% with 96% d-incorporation at the β-position, showing water is the H source

probe_reagents.name: 4-phenyl-1-butene (alkyl-tethered alkene)
probe_reagents.observation: No anti-Markovnikov hydration product 4i; epoxide 4i' isolated in 46%, indicating phenyl conjugation is required for the epoxide ring-opening/reduction step

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (Cl- vs 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl- oxidation onset 2.02 V vs SCE, below 1a first oxidation at 2.95 V; without Cl-, no 2a formed — confirming Cl- mediator role

notes: The mechanism is a chloride-mediated paired-electrolysis-like sequence: anodic generation of Cl+/Cl2 to form chlorohydrin/epoxide, followed by cathodic two-electron reductive ring-opening of the aryl epoxide. Kinetics show 6 builds up early then decays into 2a, supporting 6 as the productive intermediate. Phenyl conjugation is essential to stabilize the benzylic radical/anion formed on cathodic ring-opening.
